poster_title: Automated Evaluation of Personalized Text Generation using Large Language Models
authors: Yaqing Wang, Jiepu Jiang, Mingyang Zhang, Cheng Li, Yi Liang, Qiaozhu Mei, Michael Bendersky

content: Traditional metrics insufficient for personalized text generation.
Human judgments costly and hard to obtain.
Need for robust automated evaluation methods.
Lack of evaluation methodologies for personalization.
Difficulty in distinguishing personalization from generic text quality.
Need for scalable and efficient evaluation techniques.
AuPEL framework uses LLMs for evaluation.
Pairwise comparisons between text generation systems.
Leverages LLMs' ability in authorship attribution.
panels.figure_caption: Estimated consistency of AuPEL, human raters, and NLG metrics for comparing T5 XL vs. T5 XXL generators.

content: Validation against human annotators and traditional metrics.
Experiments with different model sizes and user profiles.
panels.figure_caption: Pairwise comparison of multiple T5 generators.

content: AuPEL outperforms human annotators and traditional metrics.
LLM evaluators improve personalization evaluation with larger models.
panels.figure_caption: Elo ratings of different checkpoints with 95% confidence intervals.

content: AuPEL is a novel and effective framework for evaluating personalized text generation.
LLMs show promise in capturing personalization nuances.
Further research needed to address remaining challenges.

Fig7.caption: Estimated consistency of AuPEL, human raters, and NLG metrics for comparing T5 XL vs. T5 XXL generators.
Fig7.description: 展示了 AuPEL、人类评估者和 NLG 指标在比较 T5 XL 和 T5 XXL 生成器时的一致性估计

Fig10.description: 展示了多个 T5 生成器的成对比较结果
